title: Fixture-id path traversal: 015 F-P1-9 guard only hardened run-benchmark.cjs, not the materializer that writes fixture outputs first
evidence: materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs:91 does fs.writeFileSync(path.join(outputsDir, `${fixture.id}.md`), ...) with no sanitization. loop-host.cjs:155-158 runs the materializer BEFORE run-benchmark.cjs, and the auto YAML step_run_benchmark mirrors this. Empirically: a fixture with id="../escaped" caused the materializer to write escaped.md OUTSIDE outputsDir (into its parent) and exit 0. The 015 regression test run-benchmark-hardening.vitest.ts:67-79 only calls runBenchmark without the materializer, so its `evil.md not created` assertion does not cover the real first-writer path; grep confirms zero materializer-traversal test coverage.
fix: Port assertSafeFixtureId / SAFE_FIXTURE_ID from run-benchmark.cjs into materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs and call it before path.join(outputsDir, `${fixture.id}.md`) at line 91 (and ideally share one helper). Add a materializer hostile-id regression test.
claim: The 014 F-P1-9 fixture-id traversal class is only partially remediated: the guard exists in run-benchmark.cjs but not in the materializer, which is the first and actual writer of fixture output files in the model-benchmark plan.
counterevidenceSought: Checked whether run-benchmark.cjs guard runs before any materializer write (it does not — separate process, runs second); checked for any sanitization or mkdir-guard inside the materializer (none); checked tests for materializer hostile-id coverage (none); ran empirical repro confirming the parent-dir escape write.
alternativeExplanation: Could be argued benign because benchmark profiles are trusted-author content, so a hostile fixture id requires a malicious/compromised profile author. Rejected as a downgrade-to-P2 because the project itself treats the analogous criteria-exec surface (F-P1-3) as a real latent surface worth gating, and the 015 packet explicitly claimed to fix this exact traversal class — leaving the first writer unguarded is an incomplete remediation, not a non-issue.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if a documented invariant guarantees fixture ids are schema-validated against SAFE_FIXTURE_ID upstream (e.g. at profile load / fixture authoring) before materialization, OR if the materializer is provably never run on untrusted fixtures in any shipped path.

title: DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC=0 hardening gate does not cover bundle-gate.cjs; fixture acceptance.command still shell-executes via the D2 smoke-run path
evidence: The 015 F-P1-3 criteria-exec gate was added ONLY in score-model-variant.cjs::scoreAcceptanceDeterministic (score-model-variant.cjs:107-113, skips execSync(a.command) for type=deterministic when DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC=0). But on the same 5dim pass, score() also spawns runDetCheck(bundle-gate, ...) (score-model-variant.cjs:209) and the virtual fixture carries acceptance: criteria.acceptance verbatim (lines 196,205). bundle-gate.cjs::scoreLayer3 (lines 128-157) selects acceptance with type smoke-run OR (deterministic AND command) and runs execSync(acceptance.command, {cwd: cwdAbs, timeout: 30000}) with NO check of DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC (the env var string is absent from bundle-gate.cjs entirely). SKILL.md:279 documents the flag as refusing criteria-driven shell execution in the 5-dim scorer; bundle-gate IS a 5-dim det-check (D2, weight 0.30, the hard gate), so the documented guarantee is false. The F-P1-3 test remediation.vitest.ts:119-131 only feeds type=deterministic acceptance through scoreAcceptanceDeterministic and never exercises bundle-gate Layer-3, so the gap is untested. Shipped default.json fixtures carry no command (dormant), but the surface goes live the moment any profile/fixture defines a smoke-run or deterministic+command acceptance under --scorer 5dim.
fix: Route bundle-gate Layer-3 through the same env gate: either (a) bundle-gate.cjs::scoreLayer3 early-returns a skipped layer when process.env.DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC===0, or (b) score-model-variant.cjs strips acceptance[].command (and skips the bundle-gate smoke-run) before writing the virtual fixture when the flag is set. Add a type=smoke-run regression to remediation.vitest.ts asserting no execution under the flag.
claim: The 015 F-P1-3 criteria-exec hardening control is incomplete: it gates one of two shell-exec paths in the 5-dim scorer (scoreAcceptanceDeterministic) but not the second (bundle-gate Layer-3 smoke-run), so a hardened deployment that sets DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC=0 still executes fixture-supplied commands, contradicting SKILL.md:279.
counterevidenceSought: Grepped bundle-gate.cjs for DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC (absent); confirmed score-model-variant.cjs unconditionally calls runDetCheck(bundle-gate) regardless of the flag; confirmed the virtual fixture passes acceptance through unmodified; read remediation.vitest.ts F-P1-3 block and confirmed it only tests the scoreAcceptanceDeterministic path, never bundle-gate; checked shipped default.json fixtures and confirmed no command field (so dormant by default, not an open shipped exploit).
alternativeExplanation: Could be argued benign because benchmark profiles are trusted-author content and the shipped fixtures define no command, so no exploit ships today. Rejected as a downgrade-to-P2: the project itself treats the analogous scoreAcceptanceDeterministic surface as a real latent P1 worth a gate, and the value of that gate is undermined if a parallel exec path ignores it. A documented security control that fails to cover its advertised scope is a real defect at the same severity as the surface it claims to protect.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if a documented invariant guarantees acceptance commands are schema-stripped/validated before reaching any det-check when DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC=0, OR if bundle-gate Layer-3 is provably unreachable on every shipped 5dim path.

title: Both Lane command docs cite a non-existent "Mode 4: Model-Benchmark" SKILL.md section as canonical source of truth; Step 1 routes the agent to "Focus on Mode 4"
evidence: start-model-benchmark-loop.md:370 (Workflow Step 1) instructs 'Read SKILL.md ... Focus on Mode 4: Model-Benchmark for the canonical contract.' start-model-benchmark-loop.md:497 and start-agent-improvement-loop.md:514 both state 'Canonical source of truth: SKILL.md "Mode 4: Model-Benchmark"'. But `rg -n "Mode 4" SKILL.md` returns zero hits. The actual SKILL.md heading inventory (rg ^# ) shows the model-benchmark contract lives under §4 'LANE B: MODEL-BENCHMARK' (SKILL.md:271); the only Mode-N headings are 'Mode 1/2/2A/3' INSIDE §3 'LANE A: AGENT-IMPROVEMENT'. The 121/009 + 121/010 reorg renamed the SKILL structure from Mode-4 to Lane-A/Lane-B but the command docs were not updated, so all three canonical-source pointers dangle. An agent following Step 1 literally cannot find the named anchor and the stale 'Mode 4' label collides confusingly with the existing Lane A Mode-1/2/3 headings.
fix: Replace all three 'Mode 4: Model-Benchmark' references with the real heading '§4 LANE B: MODEL-BENCHMARK' (or a stable anchor): start-model-benchmark-loop.md:370, start-model-benchmark-loop.md:497, start-agent-improvement-loop.md:514. Optionally add a doc-lint that command-doc SKILL.md section pointers resolve to a real heading.
claim: The live model-benchmark command body (Step 1 + Notes) and the agent-improvement command's Lane B note all point at a 'Mode 4: Model-Benchmark' SKILL.md section that does not exist after the 009/010 Lane-A/Lane-B reorg; the canonical contract is actually §4 LANE B.
counterevidenceSought: Grepped SKILL.md for 'Mode 4' (zero hits) and dumped every '^#' heading to confirm no Mode 4 exists and that Mode 1/2/2A/3 live under §3 Lane A; confirmed the model-benchmark contract is under §4 Lane B; checked the agent-improvement command to see if only one doc was affected (both are); verified the reorg packets 009/010 exist as the rename source.
alternativeExplanation: Could be argued benign because the YAML (not this prose) drives the loop, so the dead anchor never blocks execution. Rejected as a downgrade to P2 because Step 1 is an executable workflow instruction telling the orchestrating agent WHERE the canonical contract is, the pointer is wrong for BOTH lanes, and the stale 'Mode 4' label actively collides with the existing Lane A 'Mode 1/2/3' headings, so a reader can anchor on the wrong lane's content.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the command docs are demoted to non-normative footnotes that no executable step depends on, OR if a 'Mode 4: Model-Benchmark' alias heading is added back to SKILL.md so the pointer resolves.

title: Stale comment in explicit.ts claims the projection exposes a 'single canonical node (deep-agent-improvement)', but a second live node 'deep-model-benchmark' now exists and is the actual boost target
evidence: explicit.ts:123-129 comment asserts 'The projection exposes a single canonical node for that skill (deep-agent-improvement) ... The earlier alias-shaped target (command-spec-kit-deep-agent-improvement) is not a projection node, so it never reached the ranked skill and the penalty was inert.' But projection.ts:121-135 now defines a distinct id:'deep-model-benchmark' node (kind:command, lifecycleStatus:active) and aliases.ts:33-37 defines a 'deep-model-benchmark' alias group. The code at explicit.ts:130-138 correctly targets 'deep-model-benchmark' (e.g. 'benchmark a model': [['deep-model-benchmark',1.6],['deep-agent-improvement',-0.6]]). Behavior is correct; only the comment's single-node projection model is stale and could mislead a maintainer into believing the deep-model-benchmark boost is inert.
fix: Update the explicit.ts:123-129 comment to describe the current two-node projection (deep-agent-improvement for Lane A, deep-model-benchmark for the Lane B command) and note that the positive boost lands on the live deep-model-benchmark node while the bounded penalty demotes the Lane A skill.
claim: The explicit-lane comment block describing the advisor projection is stale: it claims a single canonical node where two now exist (deep-agent-improvement + deep-model-benchmark), even though the code correctly targets the second node.
counterevidenceSought: Confirmed deep-model-benchmark is a REAL projection node (projection.ts:122, lifecycleStatus active) and alias group (aliases.ts:33), so the boosts are live not inert; ran rg to confirm the code references the node verbatim; verified the comment text describes a single-node world contradicted by the projection.
alternativeExplanation: Could be read as harmless documentation that does not affect routing math. Accepted as exactly that: P2, no behavior impact, comment-vs-code drift only.

title: Four divergent parseArgs dialects; space-form support split across the in-scope scripts with no shared parser
evidence: loop-host.cjs:87-113, materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs:9-20, and run-benchmark.cjs:17-35 each support space-form `--key value` (next token consumed when it does not start with --). promote-candidate.cjs:28-38, score-candidate.cjs:35-45, and dispatch-model.cjs:359-372 do NOT: they only accept --key=value (or boolean). loop-host plans space-form args for the Lane B pipeline (planInvocation pushes ['--profile', p, '--scorer', '5dim'...]), which the materializer + run-benchmark accept; but the same `--candidate /path` typed at promote-candidate.cjs or score-candidate.cjs binds candidate=true (boolean) and the script then errors on a missing value. There is no shared arg-parse helper; the space-form contract lives only in inline comments and the loop-host vitest. Confirmed by rg: 10 separate `function parseArgs` definitions in the skill, at least 4 with materially different token-consumption rules.
fix: Extract a single shared arg parser (e.g. scripts/lib/parse-args.cjs) with one documented dialect (=-form + space-form + boolean) and have all six in-scope scripts require it, or at minimum document per-script which dialect each accepts in a NOTES block. Add a test asserting the shared parser is used.
claim: The deep-agent-improvement scripts ship at least four behaviorally-divergent parseArgs implementations; space-form `--k v` works in the Lane B pipeline scripts but silently degrades to boolean in promote-candidate/score-candidate/dispatch-model, with no shared parser enforcing one contract.
counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the =-form is the only documented public CLI surface (the command docs and YAML use space-form for loop-host/run-benchmark and =-form for promote-candidate, so each script is invoked with its supported dialect today); confirmed no current shipped invocation passes space-form to the =-only scripts, so this is a latent maintainability footgun, not an active bug.
alternativeExplanation: Could be argued intentional minimalism (each script parses only what its callers send). Accepted as P2: no behavior break today, but the divergence is undocumented and the next maintainer extending any of these CLIs has six different prior arts to reconcile.

title: Integration scoring duplicated between run-benchmark.scoreIntegration and score-candidate.scoreDimIntegration with behavioral drift
evidence: run-benchmark.cjs:317-376 (scoreIntegration) and score-candidate.cjs:276-312 (scoreDimIntegration) implement the SAME integration score: mirror starts at 100, -30 for 'missing', -20 for 'diverged', then weighted mirror*0.6 + command*0.2 + skill*0.2, command/skill binary on count>=1. Two drift points: (1) run-benchmark uses named constants MIRROR_MISSING_PENALTY=30 / MIRROR_DIVERGED_PENALTY=20 / INTEGRATION_WEIGHTS (run-benchmark.cjs:317-321) while score-candidate hardcodes `-= 20`, `-= 30`, `* 0.6` magic numbers (score-candidate.cjs:293-299); (2) run-benchmark normalizes the mirror status as `mirror.status || mirror.syncStatus` (run-benchmark.cjs:335) whereas score-candidate reads ONLY `m.syncStatus` (score-candidate.cjs:292-294). A scanner report that emits `status` but not `syncStatus` therefore scores correctly in Lane B and as all-aligned (no penalty) in Lane A. The shared algorithm has no single owner, so a future penalty/weight change must be hand-mirrored across two files.
fix: Extract the integration-score computation into one shared helper (e.g. scripts/lib/integration-score.cjs) consuming a normalized {mirrors,commandCount,skillCount} shape, and have both run-benchmark and score-candidate call it. That also resolves the status||syncStatus normalization divergence.
claim: The mirror/command/skill integration score is implemented twice with the same intended math but divergent constants and divergent mirror-status field handling, so the two lanes can score the same scanner output differently and the algorithm has no single source of truth.
counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the two consumers are meant to be independent (run-benchmark scores a benchmark integration-report; score-candidate scores an agent integration-report) — they ARE separate inputs, but the scoring algorithm is identical by design intent (same weights/penalties), which is exactly what makes the silent drift a maintainability hazard rather than two legitimately different formulas; confirmed the scan-integration scanner field name to assess whether status vs syncStatus actually diverges in practice (the normalization fallback in run-benchmark exists precisely because the field name is not guaranteed, which score-candidate does not account for).
alternativeExplanation: Could be argued the two are deliberately separate per-lane scorers that happen to look alike. Accepted as P2: no shipped functional break proven (default scanner emits syncStatus), but the duplication + the asymmetric status normalization is a real latent drift surface.

title: classifyPath has an unused fixtureCwdRel parameter
evidence: cwd-check.cjs:89 declares classifyPath(rawPath, fixtureCwdAbs, fixtureCwdRel). The body (lines 90-108) references only rawPath and fixtureCwdAbs (path.resolve(fixtureCwdAbs, rawPath), isInside(resolved, fixtureCwdAbs), rawPath.startsWith('/'/'~')). fixtureCwdRel is never read. The caller passes it at line 123 and it is otherwise only used for the `fixture_cwd_rel` echo field at line 140. rg for fixtureCwdRel inside the function body returns no hit.
fix: Drop the third parameter from classifyPath and its call site (line 123). The fixture_cwd_rel echo at line 140 already has fixtureCwdRel in scoreOutput's scope, so the result field is unaffected.
claim: classifyPath accepts a fixtureCwdRel argument it never uses, misleading a reader into thinking the relative cwd participates in path classification.
counterevidenceSought: Read the full classifyPath body to confirm fixtureCwdRel is not referenced via destructuring or closure; confirmed the only fixture_cwd_rel usage is the echo in scoreOutput at line 140 which already has the variable in its own scope.
alternativeExplanation: Could be a deliberate forward-compat hook for future relative-aware classification. Accepted as P2 dead-code/clarity: no behavior impact, but YAGNI — an unused param with no TODO is noise.

title: Cross-packet review-attribution noise: '122 review' tags carried into a 121-packet file alongside the real '014 review' lineage
evidence: dispatch-model.cjs (a 121-packet file, header says 'Generalized from 120/003 dispatch-minimax.cjs') carries a mix of attribution tags: '(014 review)' at lines 42, 50 (the actual two-lane-program review lineage this packet remediated) AND '(122 review)' at lines 117 and 264. There is no 122 packet in this two-lane program's lineage (the program is phases 008-013, reviewed in 014, remediated in 015); the '122 review' tags appear copied through from the upstream dispatch-minimax.cjs source. A maintainer grepping 'why does this line exist' will chase a review that is not part of this packet's history.
fix: Normalize the in-file review-attribution tags to this packet's lineage (014 review / 015 remediation) or, for genuinely-ported upstream rationale, label it explicitly as e.g. '(ported from 120/003)' rather than '(122 review)'. Either way make the provenance tags consistent within the file.
claim: dispatch-model.cjs mixes inconsistent review-packet attribution comments ('122 review' alongside '014 review') that do not all map to this two-lane program's actual review lineage, creating misleading provenance for maintainers.
counterevidenceSought: Checked the file header to confirm the 120/003 generalization origin (which legitimately motivates ported-rationale comments); compared against sibling files (run-benchmark.cjs, score-candidate.cjs) which consistently use '(014 review)' / F-P-N tags tied to this program; confirmed no 122 packet appears in this program's stated lineage (008-013 -> 014 -> 015).
alternativeExplanation: The '122 review' could reference a real upstream MiniMax-rig review whose fix was ported verbatim. Even so, importing the upstream packet number unlabeled into this file is misleading provenance. Held at P2: comment-only, no behavior impact.

title: fixturePathFor duplicated verbatim in materializer + run-benchmark; profiles-dir default literal hardcoded in 3 places; F-P1-4b alignment is a hand-maintained contract
evidence: fixturePathFor is defined identically in materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs:32-34 and run-benchmark.cjs:80-83 (both: path.join(dir, ref.endsWith('.json') ? ref : ref+'.json')). The profiles-dir default literal '.opencode/skills/deep-agent-improvement/assets/model-benchmark/benchmark-profiles' is hardcoded in materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs:69 (DEFAULT_PROFILES_DIR) and run-benchmark.cjs:387, and the F-P1-4b remediation (materializer comment at lines 67-68) explicitly requires the materializer's profile-by-id resolution to stay byte-aligned with run-benchmark.cjs::loadProfile by hand. The two profile-resolution code paths (materializer:71-76, run-benchmark loadProfile:69-78) are near-duplicate logic kept in sync manually.
fix: Extract a shared scripts/lib/profile-resolve.cjs exporting DEFAULT_PROFILES_DIR, fixturePathFor, and resolveProfile(profileArg, profilesDir); have both scripts import it so the F-P1-4b 'resolves identically in both steps' invariant becomes structural instead of a hand-maintained comment contract.
claim: The materializer and run-benchmark duplicate fixturePathFor, the profiles-dir default literal, and the profile-by-id resolution logic, and the F-P1-4b fix relies on those copies staying byte-aligned by hand rather than via a shared helper.
counterevidenceSought: Confirmed both fixturePathFor bodies are textually identical; confirmed the profiles-dir literal appears in both .cjs files plus test fixtures; read the F-P1-4b comment in the materializer that documents the intentional alignment requirement, which is the strongest argument that a shared helper is warranted.
alternativeExplanation: Two small pure helpers duplicated across two files is low-cost. Accepted as P2: the cost is the F-P1-4b correctness invariant being maintained by convention rather than by construction, which is exactly the kind of drift the prior review chain has had to chase.

title: Lane B promotion non-executable: promoter hard-requires a scored --score file the model-benchmark workflow never produces
evidence: auto.yaml step_promote_candidate (174-176) calls promote-candidate.cjs --benchmark-report report.json with candidate/target/SCORE/config/manifest/archive-dir, but the Lane B phase_loop only runs loop-host(materialize+run-benchmark) and reduce-state: no candidate write, no score-candidate step, no manifest copy (verified via grep). promote-candidate.cjs:153 makes --score REQUIRED and :168 rejects unless score.status===scored; the Lane B artifact report.json has status:benchmark-complete, recommendation:benchmark-pass, no top-level dimensions, no delta.total (run-benchmark.cjs:463-493). So promotion cannot run; :233 and :243 would also reject. confirm YAML:205 passes an unbound {score_output_path} placeholder.
fix: Either add a Lane B adapter mapping the benchmark report to a scored-shape --score file, OR give promote-candidate.cjs a benchmark-mode branch accepting report.json (status benchmark-complete, recommendation benchmark-pass, aggregateScore>=BENCHMARK_AGGREGATE_GATE) as sole evidence when --benchmark-report is given and --score omitted, OR explicitly mark Lane B promotion not-yet-wired and remove the executable-looking step.
claim: Documented guarded Lane B promotion cannot execute because promote-candidate.cjs requires a scored --score file (status:scored, recommendation:candidate-better, populated dimensions) that no model-benchmark step produces.
counterevidenceSought: Grepped both Lane B YAMLs for any score-candidate/candidate-write/manifest-copy/score_output_path step (none); read promote-candidate.cjs main() gate chain; read run-benchmark.cjs report and infra_failure shapes; confirmed report.json status/recommendation/dimensions shape.
alternativeExplanation: Operator might hand-run score-candidate.cjs out-of-band to produce a score.json before promoting; but Lane B never mutates a candidate agent file and has no candidate to score, and score-candidate.cjs scores agent .md files (5-dim), not model-benchmark outputs, so no in-workflow path produces a compatible score. Rejected.
downgradeTrigger: If a Lane B promotion adapter/branch is found elsewhere that converts report.json into a scored --score input, or if the YAML promote step is intended as operator-manual-only with the agent-improvement score.json reused, downgrade to P2 doc-clarity.

title: Criteria-grep arbitrary-file-read traversal is NOT covered by the DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC=0 hardening gate
evidence: scoreAcceptanceDeterministic builds the read target as path.join(cwdAbs, a.file) at score-model-variant.cjs:82 (type:'grep') and :95 (type:'grep_absent'), where a.file is fixture/profile-authored data with NO traversal guard. A criterion {type:'grep', file:'../../../../etc/hosts', pattern:'.'} reads a file outside cwd. The DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC=0 hardening gate (score-model-variant.cjs:107-113) wraps ONLY the type:'deterministic' execSync branch (line 115); the grep/grep_absent branches run unconditionally regardless of the gate. SKILL.md:279 and start-model-benchmark-loop.md:356 document the gate as refusing 'criteria-driven shell execution', so a hardened/shared-runner operator who sets the gate reasonably believes criteria-driven filesystem access is locked down, yet criteria-grep still performs arbitrary absolute-escape reads. Distinct from the prior finding 'exec gate does not cover bundle-gate.cjs' (that is the layer-3 smoke-run execSync in a different file); this is the grep file-READ path inside score-model-variant.cjs.
fix: Either (a) extend the DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC=0 gate to also disable the grep/grep_absent file reads (or add a separate DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_READ=0), and/or (b) containment-check a.file: resolve path.resolve(cwdAbs, a.file) and reject when it is not inside cwdAbs (reuse the isInside() separator-bounded check already present in cwd-check.cjs:85-87). Document the broadened gate scope in SKILL.md s4.5.
claim: The grep and grep_absent acceptance criteria in score-model-variant.cjs read arbitrary filesystem paths via path.join(cwdAbs, a.file) with no traversal containment, and the DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC=0 hardening gate that operators set to fail-closed does not cover this read path.
counterevidenceSought: Read SKILL.md:279 trusted-author rationale (fixtures/profiles authored by the operator running the loop, same trust domain); confirmed the gate at lines 107-113 only short-circuits the 'deterministic' branch and the grep branches at 81-100 have no gate and no containment check; confirmed cwd-check.cjs:85-87 already ships an isInside() containment helper, so the fix is cheap and the absence of a guard here is an inconsistency, not an architectural constraint; confirmed the read result is not exfiltrated (only a pass/match-count is surfaced into the score), which bounds the impact.
alternativeExplanation: Under the documented trusted-author boundary (profiles come only from the operator) a malicious grep file is not an untrusted-input vector, so this is latent rather than an open exploit. However the hardening gate exists precisely for hardened/shared-runner deployments where that boundary is weaker, and there it leaves criteria-driven arbitrary reads open, which contradicts the gate's stated fail-closed purpose.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the project formally scopes ALL benchmark profiles/fixtures as fully-trusted-operator-only with no shared-runner story (making the gate's read-coverage gap purely cosmetic), or if a containment check / read-gate is added so DEEP_AGENT_ALLOW_CRITERIA_EXEC=0 fails closed over grep reads.

title: Score-cache hit path trusts the filename hash and never re-validates the cached blob's own inputHash before emitting it as an authoritative score
evidence: readCachedScore (score-candidate.cjs:194-200) does fs.existsSync(cachePathFor(cacheDir, inputHash)) then JSON.parse(readFileSync(...)) and returns the parsed object verbatim. main() (lines 572-581) emits that cached object directly as the score result (status:'scored', recommendation, delta, dimensions) with no check that cached.inputHash === inputHash. The cache filename is the only integrity binding. A file forged into the packet-local .score-cache/ dir named <targetHash>.json with recommendation:'candidate-better' and a passing delta would be served as the authoritative score, which promote-candidate.cjs then consumes as its gate evidence.
fix: After JSON.parse in readCachedScore (or in main before emitting), assert cached && cached.inputHash === inputHash && cached.status === 'scored'; on mismatch treat it as a cache miss and rescore. One-line integrity check that makes a forged or corrupted cache entry fail closed rather than being served as truth.
claim: The score cache is content-addressed only by filename; the reader never confirms the cached payload's embedded inputHash matches the requested hash, so a forged or stale cache file under .score-cache/ is emitted verbatim as an authoritative score and flows into the promotion gate.
counterevidenceSought: Confirmed the F-P1-11 fix moved the cache dir packet-local (defaultCacheDir lines 171-181) rather than world-writable os.tmpdir(), which materially reduces the attack surface; confirmed the result object DOES carry an inputHash field (line 614) so the integrity check is trivially available but unused on read; confirmed predicting the SHA-256 inputHash requires the attacker to already know candidateContent + profile + manifest, i.e. they generally already control the candidate, which bounds realism.
alternativeExplanation: Given the packet-local relocation and the content-derived hash, an attacker who can write the cache file usually already controls the candidate being scored, so this is defense-in-depth rather than a new privilege boundary break. Held at P2.
downgradeTrigger: n/a (already P2); would rise to P1 only if the cache dir were ever relocated back to a shared/world-writable location.

title: "Mode-aware promotion" is an untraceable normative claim: promote-candidate.cjs has zero mode branching and is structurally an agent-only promoter
evidence: SKILL.md:278 ("apply mode-aware promotion") and :496 ("promote-candidate.cjs is mode-aware"), plus start-model-benchmark-loop.md:409 ("Step 6: Promotion (mode-aware, optional)"), :411 ("Promotion is guarded and mode-aware"), and :496 all assert the promoter is mode-aware. Reading promote-candidate.cjs in full: no mode/model-benchmark/scoringMethod token except the unrelated proposal-only error string at line 174. It hard-requires a manifest with exactly one classification:canonical agent target (line 130), runs 4-runtime agent mirror-sync via isAgentDefinitionTarget (line 256), and copies candidate over target (line 302) -- pure agent-promotion shape, no lane/mode branch. Neither the doc invocation (cmd:413) nor the YAML step (auto YAML:176) passes any --mode flag. By contrast mode-aware RECORDS are real (reduce-state.cjs:620 routes on record.mode===model-benchmark; run-benchmark.cjs:465,517 persist scoringMethod), so the docs conflate true record-level mode-awareness with non-existent promoter mode-awareness, misrepresenting promote-candidate.cjs as model-benchmark-capable.
fix: Stop attributing mode-awareness to promote-candidate.cjs. Either (a) reword SKILL.md:278/496 and start-model-benchmark-loop.md:409/411/496 to say records/reducer are mode-aware while promotion is the shared agent-canonical guarded path (and clarify Lane B promotion currently targets the same canonical agent manifest), or (b) actually add a mode/lane parameter + model-benchmark branch to promote-candidate.cjs and pass it from the YAML/doc invocations. Until one of those, the word "mode-aware" on the promoter is a spec_code traceability defect.
claim: The documented "mode-aware promotion" capability does not resolve to any shipped behavior in promote-candidate.cjs, which has no mode/lane branching and is structurally an agent-only promoter.
counterevidenceSought: Grepped promote-candidate.cjs for mode/model-benchmark/scoringMethod (only the proposal-only error string matched); checked whether any doc or the auto YAML passes --mode to the promoter (none do); verified that reduce-state.cjs DOES implement genuine mode routing (line 620) and run-benchmark.cjs persists scoringMethod, confirming the records-side claim is real and only the promotion-side claim is unbacked.
alternativeExplanation: "Mode-aware" could be read loosely to mean the promoter merely consumes a mode-tagged benchmark report (it reads benchmarkReport.status/recommendation/target). But it never reads report.mode or scoringMethod and never branches on lane, so even the loose reading does not hold; the promoter treats every input as an agent-canonical promotion.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the team confirms Lane B promotion is intentionally the same agent-canonical path and the docs are reworded to drop "mode-aware" from the promoter, leaving only a wording/clarity gap rather than a capability misrepresentation.

title: Finding-ID-shaped attribution tags in shipped source ('(traceability-3-5)', '(maintainability-8-4)', 'traceability-4-2/7-5/7-2') collide with the active 017 review's own finding-ID namespace
evidence: dispatch-model.cjs:145 carries '// P2 (traceability-3-5): emit a resume command...' and :381 '// P2 (maintainability-8-4): surface failure diagnostics...'; run-benchmark.cjs:409,466 carry 'traceability-4-2 (014 review)', :455 'traceability-7-5 (014 review)', :503 'traceability-7-2 (014 review)'. The 014 review these cite emitted EVERY finding with "id":"n" (verified in 014-review-two-lane-workflow-implementation/review/all-findings.jsonl) -- the two dispatch-model items map to real 014 findings ('Pause resume hint points nowhere' iter 3; 'Dispatcher CLI hides failure diagnostics' iter 8) but those findings never had the IDs traceability-3-5 / maintainability-8-4; the IDs were synthesized in dimension-iter-N form. That form is exactly the scheme THIS 017 review emits (maintainability-8-N, traceability-3-N), so a maintainer grepping the live 017 deltas for 'maintainability-8-4' hits a shipped source comment that looks produced by this review pass when it actually refers to 014/iter-8. Distinct from prior maintainability-4-4 (which was scoped to packet-number tags '122 review' vs '014 review' in the same file); these tags are packet-UNqualified bare finding IDs whose dimension-iter-N shape aliases an active review namespace, which normalizing a packet number does not fix.
fix: Adopt a stable, namespace-safe citation form for in-source review provenance and rewrite the 5 tags: e.g. '(014 review F-n, iter 3)' / '(014 review F-n, iter 8)' for the dispatch-model tags and qualify the run-benchmark 'traceability-N-N (014 review)' tags as e.g. '014/iter-N traceability' so no comment reuses the bare dimension-iter-N shape that an active deep-review emits. Optionally add a lint forbidding source comments matching /(correctness|security|traceability|maintainability)-\d+-\d+/ without a packet qualifier.
claim: Shipped source comments embed finding-ID-shaped attribution tags in the dimension-iter-N form (and synthesized IDs the cited 014 review never assigned), and that form collides with the live 017 review finding-ID namespace, creating ambiguous provenance for any maintainer grepping by finding ID.
counterevidenceSought: Read the full 014 all-findings.jsonl and confirmed every 014 finding used "id":"n" (no dimension-iter-N IDs ever existed there); confirmed the two dispatch-model tags map to real 014 findings by title via the 015 decision-record FIXED rows; confirmed prior maintainability-4-4 covered only the '122 review' packet-number tags in dispatch-model and explicitly asked to normalize the packet number, NOT the bare finding-id shape; confirmed the 017 review is actively emitting maintainability-8-N / traceability-3-N IDs (this very packet), making the namespace collision real and not hypothetical.
alternativeExplanation: Could be read as harmless provenance breadcrumbs that no tooling parses. Held at P2: comment-only, zero behavior impact, but the dimension-iter-N shape is precisely what a deep-review's own deltas use, so the tags are a genuine grep-collision and traceability hazard, not just style. Not raised above P2 because nothing executable depends on these comments.
downgradeTrigger: n/a (already P2); would be a non-issue if a project convention documents that in-source finding-id tags always carry a packet qualifier distinct from the active review's emit form.

title: Wired Lane B loop scores fixtures' own reference content against their own criteria; no model is invoked, dispatch-model.cjs is dead in the wired path, aggregate score is a model-independent constant
evidence: loop-host.planInvocation(model-benchmark) returns ONLY [materialize, run-benchmark] (loop-host.cjs:153-159). materialize writes each fixture's own content/markdown to {id}.md (materialize.cjs:36-58,91). run-benchmark scores that same {id}.md against the same fixture's requiredHeadings/requiredPatterns (run-benchmark.cjs:434-437). auto YAML phase_loop runs only loop-host (auto.yaml:147); no step writes model output into benchmark-outputs/{id}.md. Live: materialize+run-benchmark on default profile => aggregateScore 100, benchmark-pass, all rows 100. 5dim+llm reads the materialized reference file as outputText (run-benchmark.cjs:274), grading the reference not a model candidate. dispatch-model.cjs appears in docs/README + the loop-host RESOLVE set but is never spawned by any plan/YAML step.
fix: Insert a per-fixture dispatch step (call dispatch-model.cjs, overwrite {id}.md with model stdout) BEFORE run-benchmark scores it; OR if Lane B is intentionally fixture-self-validation, rewrite role/purpose/docs and remove the 'benchmark a model' framing + dispatch-model references; OR gate the loop so it does not emit a model verdict it never measured.

title: loop-host lists dispatch-model.cjs in LANE_MODEL_BENCHMARK resolve set but planInvocation never plans it, so its resolveScriptPath branch is unreachable
evidence: LANE_MODEL_BENCHMARK = Set(['run-benchmark.cjs','dispatch-model.cjs']) (loop-host.cjs:48-51) and resolveScriptPath maps dispatch-model.cjs to ../model-benchmark/dispatch-model.cjs (79-81), but planInvocation only ever returns materialize + run-benchmark (130-159). No plan step names dispatch-model.cjs; the lane entry and resolver branch are unreachable from loop-host. Structural footprint of correctness-9-1.
fix: Either wire the dispatch step (see correctness-9-1) or drop the unused dispatch-model.cjs entry from LANE_MODEL_BENCHMARK to remove the misleading 'it is wired' signal.

title: Grader (LLM-as-judge) D4 score open to prompt-injection / score-inflation from benchmarked model output
evidence: composeGraderPrompt (harness.cjs:57-72) embeds untrusted swe16OutputText inside a fenced block directly adjacent to the grading instruction; in a model benchmark the graded output is the adversarial axis, so a benchmarked model can emit fence-breakout text instructing the grader to return score:1.0. clampScore01 only bounds [0,1] and does not stop an inflated in-range score.
fix: Treat the graded output as data-only: harden the system prompt against injection, ignore in-output grading directives, and/or cross-check D4 against the deterministic hallucination-flag det-check before trusting the grader score on the llm path.

title: Grader cache at fixed in-repo non-run-scoped location; stale/poisoned in-range D4 trusted cross-run (F-P1-11 analogue gap)
evidence: cache.cjs:30-31 sets CACHE_ROOT=scorer/cache/ (PACKET_ROOT=resolve(__dirname,..)=scorer/), shared across every run regardless of --outputs-dir. This is the un-applied analogue of score-candidate F-P1-11 which moved its cache packet-local to avoid trusting a shared location. The grader cache-hit path (harness.cjs:201-209) only re-clamps and never re-validates the cached blob vs key inputs; the dir can be committed into the repo.
fix: Anchor the grader cache under the run/packet outputs dir (mirror the score-candidate F-P1-11 defaultCacheDir approach) and/or persist+verify the derived key inside the cached blob on read.

title: Candidate-derived command/skill refs interpolated into fs.existsSync without sanitization (path-traversal existence oracle)
evidence: scoreDimSystemFitness (score-candidate.cjs:356-363) builds cmdPath=cmd.replace(/^//,).replace(/:/g,/) then fs.existsSync(.opencode/commands/${cmdPath}.md) and fs.existsSync(.opencode/skills/${sk}/SKILL.md). commands/skills come from profile.derivedChecks.integrationPoints derived from the candidate agent file (the untrusted artifact being scored). A ref like /../../../../etc/hosts yields existsSync(.opencode/commands/../../../../etc/hosts.md): a traversal-based file-existence oracle that also perturbs resource-refs-valid scoring.
fix: Validate/normalize cmd and sk to a basename charset (reject path separators and .. segments) before interpolating into existsSync, mirroring the SAFE_FIXTURE_ID guard pattern.
